name: Navia a Tapa de Casariesgo — circular by the Arbón Reservoir
name_original: Navia a Tapa de Casariesgo — circular por el Embalse de Arbón
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Navia traverse a diverse landscape in Asturias, Spain, characterized by its blend of coastal beauty and verdant inland territories. The region features a rugged coastline with natural monuments and charming fishing villages, alongside fertile mountains, lush valleys, serene rivers, and peaceful reservoirs further inland. Cycling routes often follow the Cantabrian Sea or wind through the Navia river basin, offering varied terrain from flat coastal paths to more challenging elevated sections. Best touring cycling routes around Navia The most popular touring cycling route is Senda Costa Naviega Loop, a 15.4 miles (24.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Cantabrian Sea along the coast. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Navia a Tapa de Casariesgo — circular by the Arbón Reservoir, a difficult 39.0 miles (62.7 km) path. This route explores inland areas, circling the Arbón Reservoir with significant elevation changes. Local touring cyclists also love the Porcía Beach – Luarca Fishing Village loop from Navia, a 66.3 miles (106.7 km) trail leading through coastal villages and natural reserves, often completed in about 6 hours 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Navia is defined by coastal paths, river valleys, and rolling hills, with routes passing through fishing villages and historical sites. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate coastal loops to difficult long-distance rides with considerable elevation gain. The routes in Navia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Navia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of touring cycling routes are available in Navia?
A: Navia offers a diverse range of touring cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from easy, mostly paved paths suitable for beginners to moderate coastal loops and challenging long-distance rides with significant elevation gains. The region features a blend of stunning coastal scenery along the Cantabrian Sea and verdant inland territories with fertile mountains, lush valleys, and serene rivers.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes around Navia?
A: Yes, Navia features several excellent circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047286297/senda-costa-naviega-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda Costa Naviega Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 15.4-mile (24.8 km) trail offering beautiful views of the Cantabrian Sea. For a more challenging inland experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029983733/navia-a-tapa-de-casariesgo-circular-by-the-arbon-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navia a Tapa de Casariesgo — circular by the Arbón Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 39.0-mile (62.7 km) path exploring the Arbón Reservoir with notable elevation changes.

Q: What natural attractions can I discover along touring cycling routes in Navia?
A: Navia's touring cycling routes often lead to breathtaking natural attractions. You can cycle along the coast to natural monuments like Frexulfe Beach and the Partial Nature Reserve of Barayo. Inland, you might encounter serene rivers and peaceful reservoirs. Don't miss the chance to visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2540246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oneta Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy the coastal views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5058429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Frejulfe Beach&lt;/a&gt;. Other beautiful beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3710352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portizuelo Beach&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/812347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Moro 🏖️ La Barcue, Navia&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible.

Q: Are there any charming villages or historical sites to explore while cycling in Navia?
A: Absolutely! Navia's routes are dotted with charming fishing villages and historical sites. Along the coast, you can visit lively villages with colorful houses such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luarca&lt;/a&gt;, Puerto de Vega, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/363278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navia&lt;/a&gt; itself, where fresh seafood is a highlight. Inland, the region boasts ancient forts and traditional stone villages, offering a glimpse into its rich history, such as the Castro de Coaña. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3710397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luarca Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; is another notable landmark.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Navia?
A: Navia is generally pleasant for touring cycling from spring through autumn. The warmer months offer ideal conditions for enjoying the coastal paths and beaches. However, the varied topography means you can find enjoyable routes throughout much of the year, with lush green landscapes in spring and vibrant colors in autumn. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Navia?
A: Yes, Navia offers some touring cycling routes suitable for families. While many routes involve varied terrain, there is at least one easy route available, and many coastal paths are relatively flat and paved, making them accessible for different fitness levels. These easier routes allow families to enjoy the scenic beauty of the region together.

Q: Can I combine touring cycling with a visit to the Camino de Santiago in Navia?
A: Yes, Navia is a significant point along the Northern Way (Camino de Santiago), which is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. You can certainly incorporate a segment of this historic pilgrimage route into your touring cycling adventure. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10503651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luarca Fishing Village – Camino de Santiago loop from Navia&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 48.1-mile (77.5 km) route that allows you to experience this iconic path.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Navia?
A: The touring cycling routes in Navia are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse terrain, from stunning coastal views to tranquil inland areas, and the variety of routes available for all abilities.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are there in Navia?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes documented in Navia, offering a wide array of choices for cyclists. These include 1 easy route, 11 moderate routes, and 23 difficult routes, ensuring options for every experience level.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes from Navia?
A: For those seeking longer adventures, Navia serves as a starting point for several extensive touring cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38237553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Porcía Beach – Luarca Fishing Village loop from Navia&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 66.3-mile (106.7 km) trail that takes you through coastal villages and natural reserves. Another significant option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40361205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luarca Fishing Village – Ramón de Campoamor Park loop from Navia&lt;/a&gt;, a very difficult 100.5-mile (161.8 km) route with substantial elevation.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Navia?
A: The terrain for touring cycling in Navia is incredibly varied. You'll encounter flat coastal paths along the Cantabrian Sea, rolling hills, and more challenging elevated sections as you venture inland through fertile mountains and river valleys. Some routes feature paved surfaces, while others may include unpaved segments, offering a diverse cycling experience.
tips: View to the mouth of the river. There's also a nice beach down there that may be worth the detour
A nice pebble beach, although it is not a bathing beach. Enjoy the panoramic views. There is a pilgrim hostel next to it.
From the viewpoint of Los Cañones, or Os Cañois, you can enjoy fabulous views of the port and Tapia lighthouse, up to the curve located after the last concrete breakwater.
Tapia, capital of the council of Tapia de Casariego, stands out for its strong ties to fishing and the canning industry. Walk through its beautiful fishing and sports port.
The old town center has a historic flair and a web of narrow pedestrian streets without cars in the center of Navia.

waypoints.name: View of the mouth and beach of the Porcía River
